title: Customer Service Advisor
description:
Customer Service Advisor – Omrax Limited



We are a thriving online furniture retailer with a strong presence across multiple online marketplaces, including Amazon, Wayfair, The Range, and B&Q. We pride ourselves on offering a wide range of high-quality furniture and delivering exceptional service to our customers. Our team is friendly, close-knit, and dedicated, and we are now looking for a Customer Service Advisor to join us and help maintain our excellent reputation.



As a Customer Service Advisor at Omrax, you will be a key part of ensuring our customers enjoy a smooth and positive shopping experience. You will handle customer inquiries, resolve issues, and provide outstanding service across multiple online platforms, including eDesk, Zendesk, Mirakl, and Virtual Stock.



Key Responsibilities:



Customer Support: Respond promptly and professionally to customer inquiries about products, orders, returns, and warranties.



Issue Resolution: Resolve customer issues efficiently, including delivery delays, damaged items, or returns, while maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction.



Platform Management: Use eDesk, Zendesk, Mirakl, and Virtual Stock to track, manage, and resolve customer service requests.



Order Management: Assist with order placement, shipment tracking, and ensure a smooth purchasing experience. Experience with Linnworks is ideal.



Product Knowledge: Develop a thorough understanding of our product catalog to provide accurate and helpful information.



Feedback Collection: Gather and document customer feedback to identify areas for improvement and highlight positive experiences.



Team Collaboration: Work closely with colleagues to ensure a seamless customer experience.



Workload Prioritization: Manage multiple inquiries efficiently, know how to prioritize tasks, and work well under pressure.



Personal Touch: Communicate with customers in a friendly and professional manner, ensuring messages feel personal and engaging.



Qualifications & Skills:



Previous experience in customer service, ideally in e-commerce or online retail.



Experience working with major clients such as Wayfair, The Range, and B&Q is highly desirable.



Proficiency in eDesk, Zendesk, Mirakl, and Virtual Stock.



Experience with Linnworks is ideal.



Strong written and verbal communication skills with a friendly, professional approach.



Excellent problem-solving abilities and attention to detail.



Self-motivated, positive, and able to work efficiently under pressure.



Strong organizational skills and ability to prioritize workload.



Experience typing clear, accurate, and personable messages to customers.



A passion for delivering exceptional customer service and ensuring customer satisfaction.



If you thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment and are dedicated to providing an outstanding customer experience, we would love to hear from you.



To apply: Please submit your resume and cover letter detailing your relevant experience.

title: Head of Transformation
description:
SBFM is the fastest growing, owner operated Soft Facilities Management service provider in the UK and we are looking for an experienced Head of Transformation to join our team. 



The Head of Transformation is responsible for leading and delivering large-scale operational change across the business, driving strategic initiatives that support the company’s continued growth and operational scalability. 



A key priority of the role will be overseeing the end-to-end mobilisation of new client contracts across up to 500 sites, ensuring a seamless transition from contract award to fully operational service delivery. This includes establishing structured mobilisation programmes, coordinating cross-functional teams, and ensuring all sites are operationally ready at launch. 



Beyond contract mobilisation, the Head of Transformation will lead and deliver wider transformation initiatives across the organisation. This includes improving operational processes, implementing scalable delivery frameworks, and driving projects that enhance efficiency, service quality, and organisational capability as the business continues to scale. 



Duties and Responsibilities 



Lead complex, end?to?end transformation programmes, managing multiple concurrent initiatives while overseeing planning, budgets, timelines, risks, and overall delivery to ensure successful execution and alignment with strategic business objectives. 



Identify and deliver operational improvement initiatives that increase efficiency, consistency, and service quality across the business. 



Define project scope and deliverables while leading cross-functional teams and holding stakeholders accountable for commitments, ensuring coordinated execution and operational readiness across all departments 



Lead contract mobilisation programmes for new client wins across up to 500 sites, ensuring successful launch from contract award through to operational delivery. 



Establish scalable mobilisation frameworks and processes that support the company’s continued growth. 



Engage senior stakeholders, clients, internal teams, and external partners to ensure clear communication, transparent progress reporting, and strong alignment with project objectives and overall business goals 



Demonstrate the SBFM values and lead by example at all times. 



Qualifications and Skills 



Strong proven experience in project or programme management, ideally delivering large, complex, multi-site initiatives. 



Proven ability to mobilise or implement operational contracts within service-based industries (e.g., facilities management, cleaning, hospitality, logistics or outsourcing). 



Strong understanding of all aspects of project delivery including planning, governance, stakeholder management, risk management, and benefits tracking. 



Skilled in creating visual dashboards, Gantt charts, and executive-level presentations using Microsoft 365 tools. 



Highly organised with excellent attention to detail and ability to communicate clearly at all levels. 



Confident managing multiple priorities and navigating fast-moving environments. 



Experience driving operational improvement and transformation initiatives working with cross-functional and remote teams. 



Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. 



A people-person with a collaborative approach who thrives in high-energy teams. 



Familiarity with budgeting and financial tracking. 



Commitment to SBFM Values 



Be a Good Person 



We give people opportunities and embrace their inherent goodness. We are open minded and uphold integrity. We are respectful and considerate. We are kind and appreciative. 



Play as a Team 



Community and communication is everything. We remain inclusive within our company and with our clients. Together, we grow, retain, execute, and make an impact. We are one team. 



Think Differently 



We encourage a growth mindset, everything can be improved through effort. We love innovation and ideas. We are disruptors, and push boundaries for sustainable growth.  



Make an Impact 



It’s all about the ultimate goal. We exceed expectations and deliver results at pace. We challenge the norm and step out of our comfort zone, because at our core, we are passionate and love to win.

s

title: Remote Sales (USA Market) – Full-Time | Remote | Long-Term | Uncapped Commission
description:
Company: SecureData

Work Hours: Full-time | Night Shift (U.S. Business Hours – EST)

Location: Remote – Philippines

Salary: $1,600 per month + uncapped commission

Type: Long-term position



About Secure Data



SecureData is a trusted global leader in data recovery, digital forensics, and encrypted storage solutions. Our clients include NASA, Honeywell, Western Digital, and celebrities like OneRepublic and Pawn Stars. We’ve built strong partnerships with brands like Apple and have been recognized as the gold standard in the industry for nearly two decades.

We’re now hiring talented, English-fluent sales professionals from the Philippines to join our remote team and help us grow in the North American market.



Why Join SecureData?



-Work from Home – Full-time, stable remote role

-Uncapped Commission – Earn more as you succeed

-Paid Training – We’ll equip you to excel

-Amazing Team – Supportive, growth-focused, and fun

-Top Clients – Work with Fortune 100 companies and global brands

-Opportunities for Promotion – Grow your role over time



Your Daily Tasks



-Respond to inquiries from U.S. and Canadian customers via phone and

-Educate and assist customers with our data recovery and data protection services

-Build long-term client relationships by providing great service

-Follow up on leads and convert inquiries into closed deal

-Meet sales targets and KPIs with support from your manager



What We’re Looking For

- At least 2 years of experience in sales or customer support

-Comfortable working U.S. hours (night shift in PH)

-Friendly, confident communicator with strong listening skills

-Self-motivated and able to work independently

-Tech-savvy – experience with CRMs,
 tools, or Google Workspace is a plus

Compensation & Benefits

-Supportive, team-oriented culture



We’re excited to meet high-energy, skilled sales reps who want to grow with us long-term. If you’re ready to sell world-class services to North American customers and thrive in a supportive remote team---apply now!
